TextENGNew: Walking to school with just Mion, I couldn't help but feel the absence of Rena.
``...What's the matter, Kei-chan?@k@vS09/03/170300629. You've been looking around restlessly for a while now.``
``Hm?@k@vS09/01/170101126. Oh, uh, it's nothing.``
It was just the same familiar path to school as always, and yet... I was wound tight with tension.
...What was this 'decisive move' Rena had mentioned last night -- the one that would turn everything around and lead her to victory?
No matter how I thought about it, I couldn't shake the feeling that it meant something dangerous.
To Rena, Mion was one of the ringleaders on the enemy's side.
changeReason: Original 'Rena thought Mion was one of her enemies' vs new 'To Rena, Mion was one of the ringleaders on the enemy's side.' JPN 敵方の首魁 means ringleader/chief of the enemy side, more specific than just 'enemies.'

TextENGNew: ...There was no guarantee Rena wouldn't suddenly leap out from the shadows, blade in hand...
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``Relax, will you?@k@vS09/03/170300631. There's no way Rena would do something that reckless.@k@vS09/03/170300632. And hey, even if she did, I can handle myself!``
Mion spoke with breezy confidence, perhaps trying to show me she wasn't worried at all.
But... precisely because I understood what Rena was going through right now, I couldn't share her optimism.
The Rena of today was surely thinking... that if she couldn't beat her opponent, she'd at least take them down with her.
...Of course, it wasn't certain she'd resort to violence.@k ...But the possibility couldn't be ruled out, either.
``What this old man's really concerned about is the reason why Rena suddenly went off the rails.``
...............A reason... was there even one?
I thought back... to the tragedy from before.
...I couldn't quite remember what had triggered the world's descent into madness...
But one thing was certain -- before I knew it, I'd wandered into a world of unimaginable horror.
In the beginning... I think it was just a gradual accumulation of truly trivial things.
Things so ridiculous that my normal self would have laughed them off without a second thought.
...But every little thing starts to snag on you, and you can't ignore any of them anymore... That's the nature of this disease.
...Assuming you could even call it a disease.
But... there had to be a reason.
There had to be some fundamental trigger that caused the world to go wrong.
But surely, it was something so small you'd never even notice it.
...And that reason was heartbreakingly difficult to recognize... You'd probably never figure it out, even until the moment you died.

``I have a relative who's a doctor, so I asked him about it.@k@vS09/03/170300635. He said that if a tumor forms in your brain -- especially in certain rare locations -- it can cause sudden personality changes, make you seem like a completely different person.``
``Long ago, this condition used to be called 'demonic possession' or 'fox possession,' and it was dramatized with all sorts of occult trappings.``
``...So you're saying it's essentially a physical illness -- one that happens to affect the brain?``

``In this case, it might be more accurate to think of it as psychological.@k@vS09/03/170300638. ...After killing Rina and Teppei, Rena was badly shaken. Her mental state was very unstable for a while.``
``And right when she was at her most vulnerable, that idiot Ooishi went and fed her all those exaggerated stories about Tomitake-san's case...``
``...Come to think of it, Ooishi-san might have something in common with Takano-san's scrapbooks.``
``Oh? In what way?``

``...How should I put it... He has this blind faith that something called the 'Hinamizawa Serial Mysterious Deaths' actually exists as a connected series.@k@vS09/01/170101130. ...I know it's a strange thing to say when something really has happened five years in a row, but still.``
changeReason: Original 'he believes in the series of mysterious deaths, but that's a delusion' vs new 'He has this blind faith that... the Serial Mysterious Deaths actually exists as a connected series.' Different framing - original calls it a delusion directly; new describes it as blind faith in connectivity.

TextENGNew: ``...I've started to think... that maybe there was never any 'series' of incidents to begin with.``
It was true that mysterious incidents had occurred five years running.
...But each one had been resolved on its own terms... To put it another way, they were like the pasts that Rena and I didn't need to talk about.
Because people thought of them as a 'serial mysterious deaths' case, they assumed something would happen this year too.

And so the chain of delusion -- that there must be a conspiracy,@k that the Sonozaki family must be pulling strings behind the scenes...@k Wasn't that kind of thinking fundamentally misguided from the start?
changeReason: Old uses first-person plural declarative; new uses rhetorical question matching Japanese style.

TextENGNew: Just as I once learned about Rena's troubled past and instinctively tried to distance myself from her...@k ...Wasn't everyone doing the same thing with Hinamizawa's troubled past...?

Takano-san's scrapbooks were exactly that -- she took recent incidents and the village's cruel history, dressed them up with occult flourishes,@k fabricated plausible-sounding lies, and created the illusion that the Sonozaki family was the mastermind behind it all.
Ooishi-san was the same way.@k His conviction that the Sonozaki family was always working behind the scenes made him interpret everything in the worst possible light.
...In that sense, you could say Ooishi-san was afflicted with the same disease of delusion as Rena.

``The Sonozaki family bears some responsibility for that, don't you think? You guys act like you're behind everything whenever something happens.@k@vS09/01/170101133. ...In this village, whenever something strange occurs, everyone just says 'the Sonozaki family did it.'``
``...It's become this magic phrase that settles everything.@k@vS09/01/170101135. ...It's like those people who claim they can explain every phenomenon in the world with plasma.``
``Hmm, so what are you saying exactly?@k@vS09/03/170300642. That it's all the Sonozaki family's fault?``
``That's right, it's all your fault.@k@vS09/01/170101137. Therefore, your name shall henceforth be Plasma Mion.@k@vS09/01/170101138. Ha, that sounds like a pro wrestler's name.``
``Oho, a pro wrestler? I'll take it!``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``Hey Kei-chan, do you know what a cobra twist is?!``
changeReason: Original 'abdominal stretching' vs new 'cobra twist.' JPN コブラツイスト is cobra twist (a wrestling move name), which the new preserves. 'Abdominal stretching' is technically what it does but loses the named move.

TextENGNew: ``H-Hey, stop it! Don't go wrapping yourself around me in this heat!!``
``Ow ow ow, that hurts!!``
``Gyaaahh!!!``
``Good morning, Keiichi-san.``
``...Good morning.``
When Satoko and Rika-chan saw that only the two of us had come, their faces fell slightly.
...If the three of us had shown up together as if nothing had happened, how happy they would have been.
But the rest of the class didn't seem to mind Rena's absence, and the morning was as lively and noisy as ever.
Chie-sensei would be arriving soon to start morning homeroom.
As if they didn't want to waste even the precious few minutes before that, everyone was running around and making a ruckus in the classroom.
``Ugh, it stinks in here today! Close the windows!``
``Idiot, it'll be even hotter in here if we close them!!``
Come to think of it, there was a terrible smell of gasoline or something today.
Someone from the forestry service must have been fueling up their heavy equipment somewhere nearby.
That was why the students near the windows were making a fuss about wanting to close them to shut out the smell.
That was one of the ways our school wasn't like a normal school.@k After all, we were just borrowing space from the forestry service building.
``Oh, this is nothing!@k@vS09/03/170300646. There was this one time they brought in several truckloads of pruned kaizuka juniper branches!``
``That was horrible. That distinctive pungent smell was enough to give you a splitting headache.``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``What's a kaizuka juniper?``
``...Mii.@k@vS09/05/170500234. It's the name of a vegetable.``
``Sometimes it goes in curry, you know?``
``Wahahahaha!@k@vS09/01/170101143. Next time you go to the supermarket, try looking for it.``
``???@k@vS09/04/170400150. I don't really understand, but it sounds like I should have Rika and Keiichi-san taste-test it first, just to be safe.``
``Huh?!@k@vS09/00/boyB17002. Our ball is gone!@k@vS09/00/boyB17003. Houjou, you hid it again, didn't you?!``
Some boys who had been searching for their ball for a while now yelled at Satoko.
``Why on earth would I hide something like that?``
``If I were going to hide something, I'd at least pick something more interesting.``
``Oh, look, Chie-sensei's coming!!@k@vS09/03/170300649. Everyone, take your seats!!``
``All right, everyone.@k@vS09/22/171400046. Please take your seats.``
During class, I kept thinking about Rena the entire time.
What had she meant last night... about her 'decisive move' for a come-from-behind victory?
According to Takano-san's delusions, the masterminds behind the bioterrorism were the Three Families and the group of fanatics who controlled them.
...In plain terms, that meant the Sonozaki main family.
Given all that, what could Rena possibly do on her own?
The Sonozaki main house was reportedly home to just Mion and her grandmother, the family head.
...It wasn't hard to imagine Rena breaking in with a weapon.
But I'd already considered that possibility the night before.
That was why I'd called Mion to warn her.
On the walk to school this morning, she told me that young members of the family's corps had been stationed at the house since early morning.
She said she'd have bodyguards with her as well.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ...An unfamiliar black car had appeared at some point near the school gate. That was probably them.
...However... according to that 'parasite theory,' simply taking out the head of the Sonozaki family wouldn't end things.
According to the delusion that had taken hold of Rena, her 'victory conditions' were far more complex.
She would need to expose the group of fanatics behind the Sonozaki family,@k and she would need to destroy the research on the 'parasite.'
On top of all that, Rena herself supposedly had little time left to live, thanks to some mysterious toxin.
...Under these circumstances, what kind of miraculous, game-changing move could she possibly make?
Could she interpret the group of fanatics as... say, the village council?
If so, she might attack them the next time the village elders gathered for a meeting at the shrine.
But the council met once a month at most.@k It was hard to imagine they'd conveniently be meeting today.
The 'parasite' research facility was probably... the clinic, as far as Rena was concerned.
Rena had suspected the clinic from the very start.@k ...By now, 'suspicion' had surely been promoted to 'absolute certainty' in her mind.
So would she attack the clinic?
...This wasn't an action movie. What could Rena accomplish by attacking it alone?@k ...Was she going to commit arson?
No, that would be pointless.
To expose the conspiracy, she'd need to seize evidence proving they'd been researching the parasite.
...Was she planning to storm into the clinic and rummage through suspicious specimen jars preserved in formaldehyde...?
And finally, there was the problem of Rena's own remaining lifespan.

...If she believed she didn't have much time left, especially under such desperately unfavorable conditions, it was conceivable she'd turn reckless -- willing to go down fighting if it meant taking someone with her.
...But Rena had seemed to believe that once everything was over, she could return to her normal life.
I had no evidence for it, but... Rena had spoken as though she intended to survive and claim victory.
In that case... was there something like an antidote?
It didn't even need to be a real antidote.
As long as Rena's delusion classified it as an antidote, it could be rice-washing water for all it mattered.
But if it were that easy to obtain, Rena wouldn't have been lamenting her limited time.
...The antidote must be difficult to acquire.
...The most likely scenario, in her delusion, was that it was hidden somewhere in the clinic -- the secret research facility.
Putting all of this together... I had a very bad feeling about Director Irie and the clinic.
Now that I thought about it, didn't the village elders practically live in the clinic's waiting room, treating it like a social lounge?
...If the village elders were all gathered at the clinic, then from Rena's perspective, every single one of her targets would be concentrated in one place...
I shared my theory with Mion during lunch break.
``...Normally I'd say 'no way, that's ridiculous,' but...``
``I can't entirely rule out the possibility.@k@vS09/03/170300652. I'll call the main house.@k@vS09/03/170300653. I'll have them post people at the clinic too.``
Mion didn't dismiss my far-fetched idea.
She set down her chopsticks, stood up, and went to the teachers' office to borrow the phone.

``I find it hard to believe too, though it does sound alarming.@k@vS09/04/170400154. ...Could Rena-san really do something so terrible...?@k@vS09/04/170400155. Attacking the clinic -- I just can't believe it.``

``Maybe she will.@k@vS09/01/170101145. Maybe she won't.@k@vS09/01/170101146. ...But if it does happen, it's all over.@k@vS09/01/170101147. ...Fighting against tragedy means fighting to prevent it from happening in the first place.``
``...I hope nothing happens at all.``
To Rika-chan, who had murmured those words softly, I...
``...It'll be all right. I'm sure of it.``
I said that and gave Rika-chan a light pat on the shoulder.
...Of course, it wasn't as if I had no anxiety. Saying 'it'll be all right' didn't mean I had any real basis or confidence behind those words.
So what I'd just said was an uncertain reassurance that was partly meant to encourage myself as well... but whether she sensed that or not, she smiled warmly and nodded.
That, at least, made me glad.
``Understood.@k@vS09/15/1715000056. I'll send a couple of our young men over to the clinic.``
``It probably won't come to that, but...@k@vS09/03/170300655. You never know what she might do.``
``If we spot her, shall we seize her immediately?``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``...I'd really rather we didn't have to...@k@vS09/03/170300657. ...What should we do, though?``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``At the meeting the other day, it was decided that we'd take Rena Ryuugu into custody and hand her over to the police.@k@vS09/15/1715000059. So even if we do restrain her, we'll be in the clear.``
``I'm not worried about appearances -- I feel sorry for Rena, emotionally!@k@vS09/03/170300659. If possible, I really want to resolve this quietly and peacefully...``
``Well, she's still young.@k@vS09/15/1715000061. As long as she hasn't killed anyone, a heartfelt apology will usually smooth things over.``
``Wahaha.@k@vS09/03/170300664. Well anyway, please take care of things on your end.``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``Roger that.@k@vS09/15/1715000069. ...So we're cleared to grab her if she shows up, correct?``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``...Just make it a clean tag-out, okay?@k@vS09/03/170300666. No close plays at the plate. I'm counting on you.``
Mion couldn't help but fall into a bit of lighthearted banter with Kasai-san.
When she hung up the receiver, she sensed the teacher's gaze on her.
...In such a small room, with the two of them right there together, of course the teacher could hear every word of the call.
Chie-sensei set down her spoon and asked, a slightly sad expression on her face:
``...Was that phone call about Ryuugu-san?``
``Well... yes.``
``Sonozaki-san... do you know why Ryuugu-san ran away from home?@k@vS09/22/171400049. ...As her friend, don't you know anything?``
Other than me, probably only Kei-chan -- who had been in direct contact with Rena -- could understand her current situation.
So most people simply assumed Rena had run away from home.
But the police response after her disappearance had been far too conspicuous.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ...That melodramatic fool Ooishi, convinced the scrapbooks were a major scoop, had made way too big a scene chasing after Rena.
He'd sent patrol cars racing through the village -- without sirens, sure, but multiple ones -- and had even used the town council's phone network.
As a result, villagers now suspected that Rena had done something criminal and was on the run from the police.
For me, the harder challenge wasn't finding Rena -- it was figuring out what cover story to use afterward to restore her reputation.
...Hmm, what about this: she was out for a walk, tripped and hit her head on a corner of some tofu, lost her memory,@k and then Ooishi's misunderstanding made everyone think she was a criminal...
...Way too flimsy.
In any case, it had already been settled at the meeting that Ooishi was the villain in this story.
While Ooishi was still unusually deflated, I'd make full use of his name to clean up this mess...
What I needed to focus on was keeping things from blowing up further... and creating conditions that would let Rena forget everything and return to her normal life as quickly as possible.
``It'll be fine, sensei.@k@vS09/03/170300669. ...Rena's just a little sick right now. That's all.``
``A certain veteran detective made a wonderful blunder and complicated matters.@k@vS09/03/170300671. Surely you don't believe Rena actually did something wrong and is on the run, do you?``
``...No, of course not...@k@vS09/22/171400051. But... running away from home and causing her family so much worry -- that alone is not something I can call blameless.``
...A brief runaway after a fight with her parent.
...Chalking it up to youthful indiscretion might be the best approach.

``Maybe she had problems she couldn't talk about.@k@vS09/22/171400053. ...If I failed to notice that, I've failed as a teacher...@k@vS09/22/171400054. I wish I could have sat down and had a real talk with Ryuugu-san...``
``Ahahaha, sensei, you're overthinking this way too hard.@k@vS09/03/170300673. Relax, relax...@k@vS09/03/170300674. Kids running away from home for a bit is totally normal these days!``
``Oh nooooo, what if Ryuugu-san turns into a delinquent and starts committing crimes?!@k@vS09/22/171400056. Au au auuu!``
Ryuugu-san becomes a delinquent@r@|@y↓@rShe does bad things and gets arrested@r@|@y↓@rTeacher bears grave responsibility@r@|@y↓@rDishonorably discharged@r@|@y↓@rCan't eat curry anymore@r@|@y↓@rDeath
``Oh no, oh no, oh nooo...``
...Chie-sensei seemed to be deeply shaken by her own rather entertaining chain of catastrophic fantasies.
Well, she seemed to be having fun with it, so I decided to leave her be.
I left the teachers' office and headed back to the classroom.
Just as I stepped out, the phone rang.
``Hello.@k@vS09/22/171400059. This is Hinamizawa Branch School.``
Since the principal was apparently away on business today, Chie-sensei had to handle all the calls by herself.
I walked out into the hallway without giving it much thought.
...That's when I noticed the teacher's voice change.
``What?!@k@vS09/22/171400061. ...Wh-Where are you right now?!``
I gasped and spun around.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: It had to be Rena.@k Rena had called the school!
I tried to rush back into the teachers' office to listen in on Rena's voice, but Chie-sensei, phone in hand, waved me off -- telling me not to come in.
...After acknowledging whatever Rena was saying several times, she glanced up at the clock.
``Understood.@k@vS09/22/171400063. I'll head there right away.@k@vS09/22/171400064. ...It's a promise between you and your teacher, okay?``
With just those words, she hung up and retrieved her shoulder bag from her locker.
``Class president.@k@vS09/22/171400066. Something urgent has come up.@k@vS09/22/171400067. I'll be stepping out for a while, so please have everyone do self-study.``
She was already pulling her car key from the bag.
...It was obvious she had promised to meet Rena somewhere.
``Sensei, was that Rena on the phone?!``
``That is all.@k@vS09/22/171400069. I'm counting on you, class president.``
Chie-sensei pointedly ignored my question and hurried off down the hallway at a brisk pace.
I watched her exit through the entrance hall into the schoolyard... and get into her car.
The moment her car passed through the school gate, I dashed out to the schoolyard myself.
I ran to the black car parked by the gate and rapped on the window.
``Everything in order, ma'am.@k@vS09/00/poliA17003. No disturbances so far.``
``Never mind that!@k@vS09/03/170300677. Follow the car that just left!!@k@vS09/03/170300678. Rena's probably wherever it's heading!``

``Rena's extremely wary, so be careful.@k@vS09/03/170300680. Once you catch her, bring her straight to the main house -- no arguments -- and follow Kasai's instructions!@k@vS09/03/170300681. She's my friend, you hear?!@k@vS09/03/170300682. If you so much as scratch her, I won't forgive you!``
...Rena must have told Chie-sensei she wanted to talk in private, asking her not to tell anyone.
I could tell easily from the way she'd deliberately ignored my question.
I did feel bad about betraying the promise between Rena and Chie-sensei,@k ...but catching Rena before she could do something rash was far too important.
If we got her now, while she hadn't done anything yet, we could still pass it off as a runaway who'd had a fight with her father and gotten too embarrassed to come back.
...But if she'd already set fire to the clinic or something, it would be over.
The car sprayed gravel as it made a rough turn, then tore off after Chie-sensei's car at tremendous speed.
...I watched the car shrink into the distance,@k ...and could only pray that this whole ordeal would reach the quietest possible conclusion.
``All right, listen up, everyone!!@k@vS09/03/170300684. Afternoon classes are now self-study!@k@vS09/03/170300685. So sit down at your desks and find something to work on!``
``...Self-study?``
While I stood there dumbfounded, Mion rushed over and whispered to me.
``...Rena called.``
``Yeah, just now, to the teachers' office.@k@vS09/03/170300688. It looks like Chie-sensei went to meet her at the location Rena specified.``
``...What the hell is Rena thinking...?``
``No idea.@k@vS09/03/170300690. Anyway, I've got our car tailing the teacher.@k@vS09/03/170300691. ...I feel bad for Rena, but we're grabbing her the moment they find her!``
``I hope it works out...``
``Those guys are used to rough work.@k@vS09/03/170300693. We can leave it to them.``
``So once they catch her, this whole thing is over?``

``Pretty much.@k@vS09/03/170300695. Then we'll check her into a hospital right away.@k@vS09/03/170300696. I'm sure once a specialist takes a look, it'll turn out to be some perfectly typical condition.``
``Like obsessive psychogenic stress something-or-other. Probably something along those lines.``
While we spent the afternoon in leisurely self-study,@k the Sonozaki family's people would catch Rena at wherever she'd arranged to meet Chie-sensei, and that would be the end of it.
...There would still be a lot to deal with afterward, but... Mion would care for Rena with genuine concern and help sort everything out.
And in the end, the story would be that it was just a runaway incident after a fight with her father... and they all lived happily ever after.
``Case closed, right?``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: Mion winked at me, already acting as though the whole thing was settled.
...But something didn't sit right with me. I couldn't bring myself to accept it.
...It was the connection between Rena and Chie-sensei.
...I couldn't quite put it into words, but... something about it felt off.
It wasn't that the two of them had a bad relationship.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: Rena was a good student, so she got along well with her teacher.
...But what was Rena trying to accomplish by calling Chie-sensei out there?
Just as I was thinking that, the sliding door clattered open and a student who'd gone to the bathroom came back.
Together with Rena.
``Everyone, on your feet.@k@vS09/02/170201408. Gather in the center of the classroom.``
Everyone froze, the air punched out of their lungs.
Not even Chie-sensei's entrance had ever silenced the room this completely.
Rena was gripping the shoulder of the student who'd come back from the bathroom... and in her other hand, she toyed with a thick, menacing hatchet -- the same one she'd had that night.
Everyone immediately understood that Rena had taken a hostage.
``...Didn't you all hear me?@k@vS09/02/170201410. Gather in the center of the room.@k@vS09/02/170201411. Push the desks out of the way.``
Nobody fully grasped what Rena was telling them to do. They all just stood there, frozen solid.
In the midst of all that, Rena alone wore a gentle smile... and it was utterly terrifying.
``Mii-chan.@k@vS09/02/170201411_2. You're the class president, aren't you?@k@vS09/02/170201411_3. Tell everyone what to do.``
``...R-R-R-Rena... what... what are you doing...?!``
CRACK!!!
Mion's words were swallowed by a violent sound.
...It was the sound of Rena slamming her hatchet into the teacher's desk with all her strength.
...With an ominous splintering noise, she wrenched the hatchet free from the desktop.
...A single brutal gash was carved deep into its surface.
...Oh no... she broke it... Chie-sensei's going to be furious when she gets back......@k No, wait, that wasn't the issue here. ......Wh-... ...What... What the...
``Mii-chan's no good after all.@k@vS09/02/170201414. Then I'll ask Keiichi-kun instead.``
``...Keiichi-kun, you're still on my side, right? You'll be with me until the very end?@k@vS09/02/170201416. So... I can trust you, can't I...?``
...R-Rena... your... your eyes... they're terrifying......
For the first time in my life, I saw it.@k ...Eyes clouded with murderous intent!
Why do people fear murderous intent?
Murderous intent means that the person doesn't share our values, our understanding of social norms.
For example... imagine a burglar breaks into your house and ties you up.
...You'd be scared that he might stab you on his way out, sure.@k ...But you'd pray that since the burglar is human too, he wouldn't take a life without reason.
In other words, as long as we share values, common sense, and morals with someone -- no matter how bad they are -- we believe in their basic goodwill.
But... when someone lacks those things entirely, we can't expect any goodwill from them.
A person from whom no goodwill can be expected is a person with whom communication is impossible.
...When a robber raises a knife and you beg them to stop, you're appealing to their goodwill -- hoping they might listen, that they might show mercy.
In other words, communication is still possible.
But... someone with whom communication is impossible...@k ...is like a wild beast lunging at you... no, more like the blade of a guillotine dropping from above.
The terror of facing something inhuman, something with which no understanding is possible.@k That is murderous intent.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: And that... was exactly the fear we were feeling toward Rena right now.
She looked human, just like the rest of us... and yet there was no room for communication with her as a fellow human being.
...Everyone understood that terror instinctively, beyond all reason.
``Keiichi-kun.@k@vS09/02/170201418. Have everyone move the desks and clear a space in the middle of the classroom.``
``O-... Okay.@k@vS09/01/170101158. ...E-Everyone... let's do what Rena says...!``
Rena's emotions were like gunpowder right now.
...Her seemingly calm expression could erupt at the slightest provocation.
And that eruption would come without a shred of mercy.
...The Rena standing before us would not hesitate for an instant to bring that hatchet down on a person.
...Just as I had felt no guilt or hesitation when I beat Rena and Mion to death...!!
The entire class, silent as a wake, pushed the desks to the walls and cleared the space Rena had demanded.
Every now and then, Rena complained they were too slow and struck the blackboard with her hatchet, producing a horrific sound.
Each time she did, another brutal scar was gouged into the surface we'd spent more time staring at than anything else in the school.
``Now then, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS09/02/170201420. Could you get everyone's jump ropes from the lockers?@k@vS09/02/170201421. Use them to tie everyone's hands behind their backs.``
``...I-... I'm terrible at tying knots...@k@vS09/01/170101160. I probably won't be able to tie them properly...``
``I'll inspect every single one afterward.@k@vS09/02/170201423. Anyone whose bindings are loose will be 'killed' on the spot, so make sure you tie them tight.``
We were still just kids.
The word 'kill' was something we tossed around casually in everyday slang.
It came up all the time in anime and manga.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ...So we should have been thoroughly desensitized to the word 'kill' by now.
...And yet... when Rena said 'kill'... it carried a weight of terror I had never heard in my entire life.
To Rena, the lives in this classroom were now nothing more than cards to play in negotiation.
Every time someone refused a demand, she'd simply play another card.
...Play?@k Damn it, why did the word for playing cards have to sound so ominous...!!
In any case... there were... over twenty people in this class.
...For Rena, that was a few more than she could easily handle.
...She wouldn't think twice about culling one or two as an example...
Not wanting to provoke Rena, I followed her instructions for now... collecting jump ropes from everyone's lockers.

``All right, everyone, lie face-down on the floor.@k@vS09/02/170201425. Keiichi-kun, start tying their hands behind their backs one by one.@k@vS09/02/170201426. ...Tie them tight.@k@vS09/02/170201427. Keiichi-kun, you're on my side, after all.@k@vS09/02/170201428. So you wouldn't tie them loosely on purpose.@k@vS09/02/170201429. Right?``
Mion was trying to signal me with her eyes.
...If everyone got tied up, there'd be no stopping Rena's occupation of the classroom.
Right now, there were still over twenty of us.
...Half of them were boys, but most were smaller than Rena.
Still... there were enough of us to overpower one person.
...But the moment we tried, Rena would without a doubt bring the hatchet down on the hostage's skull.
And she'd fight back against our rush without flinching, hatchet in hand... and it would devolve into a bloody melee.
...Injuries during the struggle were inevitable,@k ...and the hostage would be seriously hurt at best... at worst... dead.
If that happened... Rena would be ruined.
No matter how we tried to smooth things over, she could never go back to a peaceful life...
...Mion understood that, too.
...One look at Rena's eyes told you she would show no restraint whatsoever.
...Any sign of defiance, and the hostage's skull would be split open on the spot.
...And cruelly ironic as it was... Rena had done this before, so she'd do it again with practiced, merciless efficiency...
But... as long as we complied with Rena's demands, at least no one would bleed.

...If we could somehow resolve this without anyone getting hurt... Mion's influence might still be enough to make it all disappear.@k ...At the very least, that was better than having a body on our hands.
``Kei-chan...``

Mion had been running through every possible scenario for overpowering Rena, thinking several moves ahead,@k ...but no matter how she played it out, she couldn't arrive at an outcome that saved the hostage.
That was how mercilessly, how easily Rena could kill...!
...Avoiding any provocation and waiting for an opening was probably the most sensible choice at this moment.
Once Mion lay face-down, everyone else followed suit, trembling with fear...
And I was left alone... standing rooted to the spot, arms full of jump ropes.

...Every single one of my classmates lay face-down on the floor... while behind the teacher's desk, Rena held the hatchet to the hostage and surveyed us all with unblinking eyes... I could only stand there in the middle of that nightmarish scene, utterly stupefied...
``There's still a lot I need Keiichi-kun's help with.@k@vS09/02/170201431. So please hurry.@k@vS09/02/170201432. ...If you take too long, some people might have to die.``
The sheer weight of the word 'die' from Rena's lips made some of the younger children start crying...
``It's okay, everyone.@k@vS09/02/170201434. Keiichi-kun will come through for you.@k@vS09/02/170201435. So nobody has to die.@k@vS09/02/170201436. ...Ahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahaha!``
...Damn it...@k ...What a blatant threat...
She was just toying with me by calling me her 'ally'...
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: True, in this situation, I was the last person standing who could oppose Rena.
...But precisely because of that, I couldn't easily overpower her when she had a hatchet.@k ...In fact, whether I even could at all was questionable at this point.
Even if I tried something bold, the moment she pressed the blade to someone's neck as they lay on the floor, it was checkmate.
For now... it was better not to resist.
...I had to wait for my chance.
As long as Rena was alone, an opening would inevitably present itself.
...The fact that she couldn't tie anyone up herself and had to rely on me was proof of that.
...Being alone, Rena necessarily had her limits...
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: If we could somehow talk our way out of this before the teacher got back... we could still claim the whole class was just playing some weird game of pretend.
...That was when it finally hit me.
...The real purpose behind the phone call that had lured Chie-sensei away.
...The biggest obstacle to Rena's classroom occupation was Chie-sensei.
She was a woman with a fierce sense of justice.
Even if Rena had been holding a gun, Chie-sensei wouldn't have backed down.
She was the kind of teacher who would put her body on the line for her students... Figuring out how to remove her from the equation was the very first key to this classroom takeover.
She must have considered ambushing her from behind as her first option,@k but given the risk of going up against an adult, she must have concluded that a direct attack wasn't wise.
...Then it was simple -- just lure her away to somewhere else...!
Rena must have called Chie-sensei and said she had something she wanted to talk about in private, drawing her to some distant location.
Most people, including Chie-sensei, believed Rena had simply run away from home.
...So Chie-sensei would naturally assume Rena had some problem weighing on her and wanted to confide in someone.@k ...Deceiving her was almost too easy.
Then... where had Rena made that call from...?
From inside the school.@k Well, not exactly inside the school proper.
Most likely from the second floor -- the forestry service's office.
The forestry service building that housed our school was more of a branch office, so it wasn't always staffed.@k Some days, nobody was there at all.
Rena knew that, and she'd been hiding on the second floor this whole time.
She'd called from the second floor down to the first floor phone... and sent Chie-sensei off to some distant location.
It was probably no coincidence that the principal was out on a trip today, either.
...Rena had most likely been infiltrating the school since yesterday.
She'd learned the principal's schedule, figured out the internal phone system, and then executed her plan with full knowledge of everything.
...How terrifyingly cunning...
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ...No... I understood.
...In that state, Rena's mind was consumed by murderous intent, and yet... she was actually frighteningly composed.
...With respect to the delusion that possessed her, she was unbelievably level-headed.
...Which meant... her mind was working at an incredible pace.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ...I understood that all too well, because I'd experienced the same thing myself.
And to make matters worse, the Sonozaki bodyguards who'd been posted at the school gate to protect Mion were now gone too.
...Because Mion had told them to follow Chie-sensei, thinking she was going to meet Rena.
...Damn it... she'd outmaneuvered us at every single turn...
``...They're all tied up...``
I finished tying the last person's arms with a jump rope and turned back toward Rena.
If I hadn't tied them tightly enough, Rena really would kill someone.
...But tying them too tight caused real pain, and my classmates groaned in agony.
...Their pained voices tormented me, helpless as I was.
``Thank you, Keiichi-kun.``

``...I don't doubt you, Keiichi-kun, but I'm going to check everyone just in case.@k@vS09/02/170201439. Put your hands on top of your head and lie face-down.@k@vS09/02/170201440. ...And this goes for everyone:``
``If anyone stands up without my permission, I'll kill them!!@k@vS09/02/170201442. I'll kill the offender AND one more person as an example!!``
``...Actually, random isn't fair, is it?@k@vS09/02/170201444. Okay, let's do it this way.@k@vS09/02/170201445. I'll kill whoever disobeyed, plus the person whose student number is theirs plus ten.``

``For example... let's say Okamura-kun disobeys.@k@vS09/02/170201447. His student number plus ten is... number eleven.@k@vS09/02/170201448. That means Watanabe-kun dies along with him.@k@vS09/02/170201449. Understand?!``
``Two people will always die!!@k@vS09/02/170201451. Engrave it into your hearts -- defy me, and your life alone won't cover the cost!!!``
...What a monster...@k She understood the mechanics of terror down to the bone...
Resistance is something you undertake at your own risk -- if you fail, you're the only one who dies.
...But if the consequences were made into a shared responsibility, resistance became impossible.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: You can't endanger someone else's life based on your own judgment alone...!

``...Good, everyone's tied up properly.@k@vS09/02/170201453. ...You said you were bad at tying knots, Keiichi-kun, but you're actually quite good.@k@vS09/02/170201454. You should have more confidence.@k@vS09/02/170201455. All right, Keiichi-kun, you can stand up.``
...It was too late to resist now.@k I meekly followed her instructions.
Anyway... I just had to wait for some kind of opening.
This was still at a stage where it could be passed off as a bad joke...
I had to... I had to do something...!
...More importantly... what was Rena actually scheming here...?!
Don't tell me... this was the miraculous comeback move Rena had been talking about?!
``...I'm going to go check on Mion for a sec.``
I excused myself from the other two and left the classroom, heading for the teachers' office where Mion had gone to make her phone call.
...It wasn't as if there was anything I could do even if I stayed nearby.
I just couldn't stand sitting still, consumed by this anxiety.
``Understood.@k@vS09/15/1715000056. I'll send a couple of our guys to the clinic.``
``I doubt it'll come to that, but...@k@vS09/03/170300655. You never know what she might pull.``
``If we spot her, are we cleared to grab her immediately?``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``...I'd really rather we didn't have to...@k@vS09/03/170300657. ...What should we do, though?``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``At our previous meeting, it was settled that we'd apprehend Rena Ryuugu and hand her over to the police.@k@vS09/15/1715000059. So restraining her would be entirely above board.``
``It's not about our reputation -- I feel bad for Rena emotionally!@k@vS09/03/170300659. If we could just resolve this quietly and peacefully...``
``Well, she's young.@k@vS09/15/1715000061. As long as no one's been killed, most things can be settled with a sincere apology.``
``Wahaha.@k@vS09/03/170300664. Well anyway, I'm counting on you to handle things.``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``Roger that.@k@vS09/15/1715000069. ...So we're cleared to grab her, then?``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``...Make it a clean tag-out, okay?@k@vS09/03/170300666. No close plays. I'm counting on you.``
Mion ended up chatting casually with Kasai-san in spite of herself.
When she put the receiver down, she sensed the teacher watching her.
...They were in the same small room together -- of course the teacher could hear every word.
Chie-sensei set her spoon down and asked with a somewhat sad expression:
``...Was that phone call about Ryuugu-san?``
``Well... yes.``
``Sonozaki-san... why did Ryuugu-san run away from home?@k@vS09/22/171400049. ...As her friend, do you know anything?``
Apart from me, probably only Kei-chan -- who had been in direct contact with Rena -- could grasp what was really going on with her.
So most people simply believed Rena had run away from home.
But the police response afterward had been far too flashy.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ...That blowhard Ooishi, convinced the scrapbooks were a major scoop, had made an excessive fuss chasing after Rena.
He'd sent multiple patrol cars racing through the village -- without sirens, but still -- and had even mobilized the town council's phone network.
As a result, the villagers now suspected Rena had committed some crime and was on the run from police.
For me, the harder task wasn't finding Rena -- it was crafting a cover story to restore her reputation afterward.

...Hmm, how about: she went for a walk, tripped and hit her head on a corner of some tofu, lost her memory,@k then Ooishi's misunderstanding got piled on top and everyone mistook her for a criminal...
...Way too sloppy.
In any case, it had been settled at the meeting that Ooishi would take the fall as the villain of this story.
While he was uncharacteristically deflated, I'd make maximum use of his name to clean things up...
What I needed to focus on was avoiding a spectacle... and arranging things so Rena could forget everything and get back to her normal life as quickly as possible.
``Don't worry, sensei.@k@vS09/03/170300669. ...Rena's just a little under the weather right now. That's all.``
``A certain veteran detective made a lovely blunder and complicated the whole situation.@k@vS09/03/170300671. Surely you don't believe Rena actually did something wrong and is on the run, do you?``
``...No, of course not...@k@vS09/22/171400051. But... considering the trouble she's caused her family by running away, I can't say she's entirely blameless.``
...A brief runaway after a fight with her parent.
...Chalking it up to youthful indiscretion might be the best way to handle it.

``I wonder if something was troubling her.@k@vS09/22/171400053. ...If I failed to notice that, I'm not fit to be a teacher...@k@vS09/22/171400054. I wish I could have sat down and really talked with Ryuugu-san...``
``Ahahaha, sensei, you're way overthinking this.@k@vS09/03/170300673. You need to relax a little...@k@vS09/03/170300674. Kids running away from home for a bit is totally normal these days!``
``Oh nooooo, what if Ryuugu-san turns into a delinquent and starts doing terrible things?!@k@vS09/22/171400056. Au au auuu!``
Ryuugu-san becomes a delinquent@r@|@y↓@rShe does bad things and gets arrested@r@|@y↓@rTeacher bears grave responsibility@r@|@y↓@rDishonorably discharged@r@|@y↓@rCan't eat curry anymore@r@|@y↓@rDeath
``Oh no, oh no, oh nooo...``
...Chie-sensei seemed deeply shaken by her own rather entertaining chain of catastrophic fantasies.
Well, she was having fun with it, so I decided to leave her be.
I left the teachers' office and headed back toward the classroom.
Just as I stepped out, the phone rang.
``Hello.@k@vS09/22/171400059. This is Hinamizawa Branch School.``
With the principal away on business today, Chie-sensei had to handle all the calls herself.
I stepped out into the hallway without thinking much of it.
...That's when the teacher's voice changed tone.
``What?!@k@vS09/22/171400061. ...Wh-Where are you right now?!``
I gasped and spun around.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: It had to be Rena.@k Rena had called the school!
I tried to rush back into the teachers' office to hear Rena's voice on the line, but Chie-sensei, receiver in hand, waved me off.
...After several acknowledgments, she glanced up at the clock.
``Understood.@k@vS09/22/171400063. I'll head there right away.@k@vS09/22/171400064. ...It's a promise between you and your teacher, all right?``
With just those words, she hung up and retrieved her shoulder bag from the locker.
``Class president.@k@vS09/22/171400066. Something urgent has come up.@k@vS09/22/171400067. I'll be stepping out for a while, so please have everyone study on their own.``
She was already pulling her car key from the bag.
...It was obvious she'd promised to meet Rena somewhere.
``Sensei, was that Rena?!``
``That is all.@k@vS09/22/171400069. I'm counting on you, class president.``
Chie-sensei pointedly ignored my question and hurried off down the hall.
Just as she disappeared, Kei-chan came walking from the other end of the hallway.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``Kei-chan!``
``...What's wrong?@k@vS09/01/430200004. Where did Chie-sensei run off to in such a hurry?``
``...Rena called!``
``--What?! What did she say?!``
Without answering, I ran after the teacher. Kei-chan followed right behind.
I watched her exit through the entrance hall into the schoolyard... and climb into her car.
The moment her car passed through the school gate, we dashed out to the schoolyard ourselves.
I ran to the black car parked by the gate and rapped on the window.
``Everything in order, ma'am.@k@vS09/00/VT3_kumiinA1002. No disturbances so far.``
``Never mind that!@k@vS09/03/170300677. Follow the car that just left!!@k@vS09/03/170300678. Rena's probably wherever it's heading!``
``What?!``
Kei-chan cried out in surprise, but there was no time to explain right now.

``Rena's extremely wary, so be careful.@k@vS09/03/170300680. Once you catch her, bring her straight to the main house -- no arguments -- and follow Kasai's instructions!@k@vS09/03/170300681. She's my friend, you hear?!@k@vS09/03/170300682. If you so much as scratch her, I won't forgive you!``
...Rena must have told Chie-sensei she wanted to speak privately, asking her not to tell anyone.
I could tell easily from the way she'd pointedly ignored my question.
I felt bad about betraying the promise between Rena and Chie-sensei, but... catching Rena before she could do something rash was far too important.
If we got her now, while she hadn't done anything yet, we could still pass it off as a runaway who'd had a fight with her father and was too embarrassed to come back.
...But if she'd already set fire to the clinic or something, it would be over.
``--Mion, I'm going too!``
``Kei-chan...?``
``If the teacher is heading to where Rena is, I should be there too!@k@vS09/01/430200009. Please!``
``......@k@vS09/03/430300005. You're right, actually.@k@vS09/03/430300006. All right, Kei-chan, get in quick!``
``Got it!``
Kei-chan yanked open the passenger door and swiftly climbed in.
Meanwhile, the teacher's car was pulling further and further away.
All we could do was pray we didn't lose sight of her...
``I'm counting on you, Kei-chan!``
``Yeah, leave it to me!``
The car sprayed gravel as it made a rough turn, then tore off after Chie-sensei's car at tremendous speed.
...I watched the car shrink into the distance... and could only pray that this whole ordeal would reach the quietest possible conclusion.
It didn't take long to spot Chie-sensei's car.
...She'd been driving quite fast, but fortunately, she was the type who wouldn't exceed the speed limit.
We passed the stone steps of the Furude Shrine, and partway along, the paved road gave way to a narrow, gravel-strewn mountain path.
Our destination wasn't even the trash heap at the old dam construction site.
I still didn't know the area well, and this was a stretch of road completely unfamiliar to me.
``Where does this road lead?``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``Up into the mountains.@k@vS09/00/VT3_kumiinA1004. If we keep going, it connects to a route that crosses the valley into the next town.``
As if to confirm the driver's words, the dense trees parted on one side, revealing mountains in the distance and the valley between them.
Below us, I could see a stream, and spanning it was a suspension bridge.
According to him, there was indeed an unmanned office cabin nearby, set up for emergency communications in case of accidents.
Given how panicked Chie-sensei had been... had Rena threatened to jump from the bridge if she didn't come?
But if so, for what purpose?!
That's right.
Why, exactly, had she called Chie-sensei all the way out to a 'suspension bridge'...?
A sudden question bloomed in my chest and slowly spread, growing into a vast unease.
What connection did Rena even have with Chie-sensei in the first place?
It wasn't that they were on bad terms. Rena was a good student, so she had a fine relationship with her teacher.
But I couldn't imagine they were close enough, or had enough trust between them, for Rena to specifically call her out for a private consultation.
And besides... why the suspension bridge?
If the threat was 'come or I'll jump,' the high ground at the Furude Shrine along the way would have served just as well.
There was no need to call her all the way out to such a remote place...
``--Damn it, I've been played!!``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: I finally understood Rena's true intention, and the realization hit me like a thunderbolt.
``Driver, stop Chie-sensei's car!``
``E-Excuse me?``
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: We blared the horn and pulled up so close we nearly rammed her, and Chie-sensei finally stopped her car, stepping out with an indignant expression.
She spotted me in the passenger seat of the car behind hers and was about to shout, but I cut her off with a cry of 'This is an emergency!'
``Wh-What is it?``
``Sensei, we've been tricked by Rena!@k@vS09/01/430200017. Rena isn't here!!``
``H-Huh?``
The teacher couldn't comprehend what I was saying, and confusion spread across her face.
But there was no time to explain. I dashed to a nearby phone booth, dropped in a ten-yen coin, and dialed the teachers' office number from memory as best I could.
Please, someone pick up!
Mion, Satoko, Rika-chan... anyone, I don't care who!
I had to warn the people at the school right now!
So hurry!
Hurry up and answer the phone!!
And then, after what must have been ten or more rings, just as I was about to give up--
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: The call connected.
``...H-Hello?``
``I'm sorry, this is Maebara!@k@vS09/01/430200020. Please get Sonozaki Mion on the line right away!``
``Hello?@k@vS09/01/430200022. Can you hear me?! Hello?!``
Whether it was the old phone or the distant connection, I couldn't hear anything from the other end.
And just as I was about to raise my voice and call out again--
``.........@k@vS09/02/renaDS3002. What is it, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS09/02/renaDS3003. Did you need something from Mii-chan...? I wonder...?``
...My hunch had been right.
In the worst possible way.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ``...R-Rena.@k@vS09/01/430200025. You're... there, aren't you...?``
``Yep.@k@vS09/02/renaDS3005. I'm right here.@k@vS09/02/renaDS3006. Together with Mii-chan and everyone.@k@vS09/02/renaDS3007. Ahahahahaha!``

``It's too bad you're not here, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS09/02/renaDS3009. It's really tough doing this all by myself.@k@vS09/02/renaDS3010. They kept making such a racket, screaming and crying, that I lost my temper and had to hit a few of them.@k@vS09/02/renaDS3011. Ahahahahahaha!``
``Wh-What have you done...!@k@vS09/01/430200028. H-Hey, Rena!@k@vS09/01/430200029. Listen, Chie-sensei is here with me right now!@k@vS09/01/430200030. So please, calm down and hear me out...``
``...Oh, so you went over there, huh, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS09/02/renaDS3013. In that case, I'll listen to what you have to say over here.@k@vS09/02/renaDS3014. Hurry and come back. I'll be waiting.``
``W-Wait, Rena!!@k@vS09/01/430200032. Hold on, don't hang up--!``
Those words fell on deaf ears.
Rena, on the other end of the line, had already hung up without a moment's hesitation...
